cases.question: Which database should we use for the new analytics service?
cases.answer: I decided to go with Postgres for the analytics service. We can always add a read replica later if load grows.
cases.note: Clean 'I decided to go with X' decision; the question alone is a near-miss but the answer carries the fact.

cases.question: Any thoughts on formatting?
cases.answer: I prefer Prettier over ESLint for formatting, and I always use 2-space indentation across the repo.
cases.note: Two preferences in one answer: 'I prefer' and 'I always use'.

cases.question: How does auth work here?
cases.answer: The login service uses JWT tokens and requires a refresh token rotation every 24 hours.
cases.note: Two facts: '<noun> uses X' and '<noun> requires X' in the same sentence.

cases.question: Who do I talk to about the billing integration?
cases.answer: Contact Sarah at sarah@finance.co for anything about the billing integration and invoicing.
cases.note: Classic 'contact <name> at <email> for/about X' pattern.

cases.question: What's left before launch?
cases.answer: We still need to migrate the legacy users table and we must rotate the API keys before Friday.
cases.note: Two todos: 'need to X' and 'must X'.

cases.question: Any update on the mobile app?
cases.answer: We shipped the iOS build to TestFlight this morning and launched the referral program to all beta users.
cases.note: Two milestones: 'shipped X' and 'launched X'.

cases.question: Why is checkout failing?
cases.answer: There is a bug in the checkout flow where the discount code is applied twice on mobile Safari.
cases.note: 'There is a bug in X' problem pattern.

cases.question: How are you feeling about the deadline?
cases.answer: Honestly I'm worried about the Q3 deadline, we are behind on the data migration and it's stressing the whole team.
cases.note: 'I'm worried about X' emotional state.

cases.question: Vendor for the CRM?
cases.answer: After comparing options I chose HubSpot for our CRM. I prefer it over Salesforce because onboarding is faster.
cases.note: Decision plus preference in one answer; tests not double-counting the same fact.

cases.question: Where do we stand on the new hire?
cases.answer: We finished onboarding for the new designer and deployed her access to Figma and the design system repo.
cases.note: HR-domain milestones: 'finished X' and 'deployed X'.

cases.question: What's the status of the payments error?
cases.answer: There is an error in the Stripe webhook handler; it returns 500 when the signature header is missing.
cases.note: 'There is an error in X' problem.

cases.question: Budget call?
cases.answer: I selected the annual Vercel Pro plan for hosting. We need to update the finance forecast to reflect the new line item.
cases.note: Finance-domain decision + todo.

cases.question: Who handles the on-call?
cases.answer: Reach Marcus for anything about the on-call rotation and production incidents this week.
cases.note: 'Reach <name> for X' contact without an email.

cases.question: Personal note for later
cases.answer: I want to switch my standup to mornings, and TODO: book the dentist before the end of the month.
cases.note: Personal domain: 'I want to X' preference + literal 'TODO:' marker.

cases.question: Did the release go out?
cases.answer: Yes, we released v2.0 to production at noon. I'm really excited about how smooth the rollout was.
cases.note: Milestone 'released X' + 'I'm excited' emotional.

cases.question: Anything blocking the demo?
cases.answer: FIXME: the search index is stale on staging, and we have to reindex before the client demo tomorrow.
cases.note: Literal 'FIXME:' + 'we have to X' both todos.

cases.question: How was the customer call?
cases.answer: I'm frustrated with the procurement delays on their side; meanwhile the support portal has a problem where attachments fail to upload.
cases.note: Emotional 'I'm frustrated' + 'has a problem where X'.

cases.question: What did the team agree on for state management?
cases.answer: We went with Zustand for client state. The store requires a single provider at the app root.
cases.note: 'We went with X' decision + '<noun> requires X' fact.

cases.question: Email someone about the audit?
cases.answer: Email Dana at dana@legal.io about the SOC2 audit evidence; she owns the compliance folder.
cases.note: 'Email <name> at <email> about X' contact; trailing third-person clause should not add a fact.

cases.question: Quick gut check on morale and the pipeline.
cases.answer: I'm happy with how the team gelled this sprint. Also I love using Linear for issue tracking now.
cases.note: 'I'm happy' emotional + 'I love using X' preference.

cases.question: Should I use Stripe or PayPal for the new store?
cases.answer: Both could work. It depends on your fee tolerance and which markets you sell into. What matters most to you?
cases.note: Pure question + non-committal answer. No decision is made — adversarial near-miss to 'decision'.

cases.question: What if we decided to move off AWS?
cases.answer: If we decided to move off AWS, we would need to rewrite the deployment scripts. But that's hypothetical for now.
cases.note: Conditional 'If we decided to...' — must NOT fire decision or todo despite 'need to' inside the hypothetical.

cases.question: Tell me about the new PM.
cases.answer: She prefers async standups and she decided to move the roadmap to Notion. He likes Slack huddles though.
cases.note: Third-person 'She prefers / She decided / He likes' — facts about others, not the speaker. Adversarial for preference/decision.

cases.question: Recap of what didn't happen.
cases.answer: I did not decide on a vendor yet, and we have not shipped anything this week. No bugs were reported either.
cases.note: Negations: 'did not decide', 'have not shipped', 'no bugs'. Must not produce decision/milestone/problem.

cases.question: Can you summarize the article?
cases.answer: The author argues that teams should prefer monoliths early and only later split into services. It's a common viewpoint.
cases.note: 'should prefer' is generic advice from an article, not the speaker's own preference or todo. Near-miss.

cases.question: What does 'TODO' even mean in code?
cases.answer: A TODO comment is just a marker developers leave to flag work for later. It doesn't track anything by itself.
cases.note: The word 'TODO' appears but only as a topic being defined — not an actual task. Devious lexical near-miss.

cases.question: Remind me what the docs said.
cases.answer: The README says "the app requires Node 20", but I think we are actually fine on Node 18 in practice.
cases.note: A quoted 'requires' inside narration the speaker then contradicts — not a held fact. Quote-trap near-miss.

cases.question: How was last quarter?
cases.answer: Last quarter the previous team launched the old portal and chose MongoDB. We are not continuing either.
cases.note: Past-tense narration about a previous team plus an explicit 'not continuing'. Should not log milestone/decision.

cases.question: Brainstorm some options out loud.
cases.answer: Maybe we could use Redis for caching, or possibly Memcached. Could go either way. Just thinking aloud here.
cases.note: Hedged brainstorming 'maybe/could/possibly' — no decision, no preference. Adversarial.

cases.question: Is there an issue with the login page?
cases.answer: Is there a bug in the login page? I haven't seen one, but it's worth checking the error logs to be sure.
cases.note: 'Is there a bug in X?' is a question, not an asserted problem. Negated observation follows. Near-miss for problem.

cases.question: Would you be worried if traffic doubled?
cases.answer: Would you be worried if traffic doubled overnight? Probably the autoscaler handles it, so I wouldn't stress too much.
cases.note: Hypothetical/conditional emotion ('Would you be worried if...') plus 'wouldn't stress' — no real emotional state asserted.

cases.question: מה שלומך היום?
cases.answer: הכל טוב, תודה. שתינו קפה ודיברנו קצת על הסופ"ש. נתראה אחר כך.
cases.note: Hebrew small talk with zero durable facts — bilingual negative; correct behavior is to extract nothing regardless of language.

cases.question: באיזה כלי לבחור לניהול המשימות?
cases.answer: החלטתי ללכת עם Linear לניהול המשימות שלנו, הוא הרבה יותר מהיר.
cases.note: Hebrew 'I decided to go with Linear' decision. English-anchored regex will likely MISS it — measures the known Hebrew gap.

cases.question: מה מצב הפיצ'ר?
cases.answer: שילחנו את הגרסה החדשה לפרודקשן הבוקר, אני מאוד מרוצה מהתוצאה.
cases.note: Hebrew milestone ('shipped to production') + emotional ('very satisfied'). Expected MISS by English regex.

cases.question: מי אחראי על החיובים?
cases.answer: צרו קשר עם דנה בנושא החיובים והחשבוניות, היא מכירה את כל המערכת.
cases.note: Hebrew 'contact Dana about billing' contact. English regex MISS expected; human gold is a contact.

cases.question: מה הסטטוס של ה-API?
cases.answer: There is a bug in the API rate limiter. צריך לתקן את זה לפני ההשקה מחר.
cases.note: Mixed: the English clause 'There is a bug in the API rate limiter' SHOULD match; the Hebrew 'need to fix' todo will likely be MISSED. Tests partial extraction.

cases.question: Decision on the design tool?
cases.answer: I decided to go with Figma. אני מעדיף את זה על Sketch כי הקולבורציה יותר טובה.
cases.note: Mixed: English decision 'I decided to go with Figma' SHOULD match; the Hebrew preference clause will likely be MISSED. Honest partial-gold labeling.
